claims_verified.claim: Round-5 change: a write-time OSError now unlinks the empty O_CREAT file (best-effort) before raising, restoring the 'nothing written' invariant
claims_verified.evidence: note_server/writer.py:79-89 — `except OSError: try: os.unlink(path) except OSError: pass; raise WriteError(...)`; fd is already closed by the with-block unwinding before unlink, so no fd leak

claims_verified.claim: The derelict-file fix prevents a failed write from silently spilling a retry onto {name}-2.md
claims_verified.evidence: test_writer.py:93-116 (test_write_note_failed_write_does_not_shift_retry_to_suffix): after a mocked ENOSPC failure the folder is empty and a retry reuses notes.md, with notes-2.md absent; passes

claims_verified.claim: Failed write leaves the folder empty (no debris)
claims_verified.evidence: test_writer.py:92-93 assertion `list(tmp_path.iterdir()) == []` after the write-time OSError; passes

claims_verified.claim: Full suite, ruff, and basedpyright all pass
claims_verified.evidence: pytest: 713 passed in 80.80s; note_server subset: 55 passed; ruff: All checks passed!; basedpyright: 0 errors, 1574 warnings (errors are the gate)

claims_verified.claim: Prior blocker (OS write failure leaking absolute path) remains fixed
claims_verified.evidence: note_server/writer.py:71-89 wraps both os.open and fdopen/write OSErrors into path-free WriteError; handler test_server.py:108-144 and end-to-end call_tool test assert no path leak; all pass

claims_verified.claim: TOTP is standard RFC 6238 with correctly-decoded hardcoded secret
claims_verified.evidence: Independent recompute confirmed earlier: KNVWKZLWFVHWW2LOMF3WC -> b'Skeev-Okinawa'; ts 0/59/1234567890 -> 411400/795119/948172 matching test_totp.py:34-37

claims_verified.claim: project_name guard, no-clobber dedup, config-via-env, single-tool surface all unchanged and covered
claims_verified.evidence: note_server/writer.py:12,26-28,60-81; config.py:31-61; test_server.py:28-35; all corresponding tests pass

claims_verified.claim: mcp is the only runtime dep and basedpyright include covers note_server

test_assessment: All acceptance criteria remain covered and the Round-5 delta is well-tested. AC1 (tool surface) by test_registers_exactly_one_save_note_tool; AC2 (TOTP accept/reject + no-write-on-reject + reference vector) by test_totp.py:45-77 and test_server.py:65-79; AC3 (project_name guard incl. traversal) by test_writer.py:20-26 and test_server.py:85-93; AC4 (happy path verbatim) by test_happy_path_writes_verbatim_and_returns_filename; AC5 (no-clobber dedup) by test_writer.py:34-45; AC6 (no path leak across auth/validation/write-error) by test_writer.py:60-92 and test_server.py:108-144 at writer, handler, and end-to-end levels; AC7 (config via env) by test_note_config.py; AC8 (green suite + dep hygiene) verified. write_note's error path now additionally guarantees the 'nothing written' invariant on a write-time failure, covered by two new tests (empty-folder assertion + retry-reuses-base-name). Every new function has failure-mode tests including error paths, boundary/empty inputs, adversarial names, and OS-failure branches. No blocker or advisory findings remain.
residual_risks: The best-effort os.unlink on write failure ignores unlink errors by design (the original OS error is what is reported); in the rare case the unlink itself fails, an empty file could remain and a retry would dedup to {name}-2.md — acceptable and honestly commented.
The path-escape defence-in-depth branch (writer.py:64-65) stays unreachable via the public API given the regex, so it remains inherently untestable without bypassing validation — acceptable.
